content.problemStatement:
There are NNN chits, out of which, AAA chits have 111 written on them and BBB (A+B=N)(A+B = N)(A+B=N) chits have 000 written on them.

Chef and Chefina are taking alternate turns. They both have a personal score which is initially set to 000. Chef starts first. On their turn, a player will:


Pick a random chit among the available chits;
Add the number written on the chit to their personal score;
Remove the chit that was picked.
Determine the expected score of Chef after all chits have been picked.

The expected score can be written in the form of PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP​ such that QQQ is not divisible by 998244353998244353998244353 and its guaranteed that for testcases that QQQ is not a multiple of 998244353998244353998244353. Output the value of P⋅Q−1P \cdot Q^{-1}P⋅Q−1 mod 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two integers AAA and BBB, the number of chits having 111 and 000 respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output the expected score of Chef.

The expected score can be written in the form of PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP​. Output the value of P⋅Q−1P \cdot Q^{-1}P⋅Q−1 mod 998244353998244353998244353.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers A and B — the number of chits with value 1 and the number of chits with value 0 respectively. It is guaranteed that A+B ≥ 1.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer – the expected score of Chef modulo 998244353, i.e. P·Q⁻¹ mod 998244353 where the expected value is expressed as the fraction P/Q in lowest terms.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: N=2 (even). Expected score = A/2 = 1/2. 1 * 2^{-1} mod 998244353 = 499122177.
Test case 2: A=0, so expected score is 0.
Test case 3: N=7 (odd), Chef gets ceil(7/2)=4 turns. Expected = 3*4/7 = 12/7. 12 * 7^{-1} mod 998244353 = 285212674.

solution.approach: The order in which the chits are drawn is a uniformly random permutation. Chef picks the 1st, 3rd, 5th … positions, i.e. exactly ceil(N/2) chits where N = A+B. By symmetry each of the N chits is equally likely to occupy any position, therefore the probability that a particular ‘1’ chit is taken by Chef equals ceil(N/2)/N. The expected number of ‘1’ chits Chef obtains is A·ceil(N/2)/N. The answer is this rational number modulo MOD = 998244353. Compute numerator = A * ceil(N/2) (mod MOD) and denominator = N (mod MOD). The required value is numerator * inv(denominator) mod MOD, where inv(x) = x^{MOD‑2} mod MOD (Fermat’s little theorem). The algorithm runs in O(log MOD) per test case for the modular exponentiation.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T·log MOD) – each test case performs one fast exponentiation.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few 64‑bit variables are stored.
